summary: SanctumApplicationTest meets every Task 5 requirement. Both Decision-4 branches are covered by tests whose names mirror the task's TDD Anchors (installsCrashHandlerInMainProcess, skipsInstallInCrashProcess). @Before / @After is symmetric: it snapshots and restores BOTH Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler() AND ActivityThread.mBoundApplication.processName, preventing JVM-global leakage into neighboring Robolectric suites — a concern called out in tech-spec Task 5 edge cases. The setUp also plants a private SentinelHandler before each test so guard-path assertions are unambiguous regardless of what an earlier Robolectric class leaked. Reflection into android.app.ActivityThread is necessary and justified: SanctumApplication is final + @HiltAndroidApp and tasks/5.md line 40 explicitly forbids subclassing for testability; Robolectric 4.12 has no first-class public shadow for Application.getProcessName() — the reflection path targets the same backing field the real getProcessName() reads on API 28+, documented in the test's KDoc (lines 15-27). Assertions are strong on both branches: main-process test uses `is CrashHandler` (meaningful type check, not mere non-null) with a descriptive failure message showing the actual class name; crash-process test combines assertFalse(is CrashHandler) with assertSame(SentinelHandler, after), precisely proving the guard skipped install (not just 'didn't install a CrashHandler'). Litmus test passes on both cases: removing `installCrashHandler()` fails the main-process test; removing the `getProcessName() == packageName` guard fails the crash-process test (both assertFalse and assertSame). Hand-rolled SentinelHandler object only — no Mockito/MockK — consistent with ErrorLogTest and the RecordingKiller pattern from Task 2. No simpler path to stub getProcessName() was missed given tech-spec constraints.
